Continue development?:
For the sports one I would
not for the matching one, which i'd rather start from zero.
Needed improvements:
domain model dirst. I need to adjust the entities ,relationships and attributes.
then the pages layout and logic flows
Additional comments: The sports one is much more better than the matching one. It seems to me that there are always two roles appear in on user story in the matching one which could make LLM confused.

Strongest aspects: The domain model seems to include the different elements presented in the user stories (entities, attributes, some associations). That saves a lot of time, which is great
Missing/incorrect elements: Missing associations between entities, too many pages without a function (costs time to remove them)
Continue development?: Yes, creating all the entities and their attributes for the domain models is a lot of work (depending on de situation), so if you can do that automatically, that would help me.
Needed improvements: Improve the associations; feels like a quick fix

Strongest aspects:
1. The domain models create the correct entities with the user stories
2. The parameters in the workflows are correct and it names the right ones
3. The pages have the correct buttons per action.
Missing/incorrect elements:
1. Pages are mostly empty
2. Workflows could improve with having more actions instead of just naming them
3. Entities in the domain models mostly have little to no relationship between them.
Continue development?: I would use it as a first thought process. I like that it already gives you a bump in the correct direction, especially with the parameters and possible workflows.
Needed improvements: Relationships in domain model and more extensive workflows. Or maybe a plan for what the workflow is for.
Additional comments: I like the research and it is interesting to explore the possibilties.

Strongest aspects: '- the domain model, the generated objects without properties
Missing/incorrect elements:
'- incorrect relations
- property validations
- blank pages
- useless microflows
Continue development?: Sure there is already a domainmodel, where i can build on top of it
Needed improvements:
fix the relationships inside the domain model
added validation to the properties of domain objects
add more properties to the domain objects
Additional comments: This tool is really use full for the generation of domain models. My advies is focus on the generation of the domainmodel and remove the functionality to generate page, nanoflow etc..

Strongest aspects: The generated entity models contain most of the details that were present in the user stories and recognized the most important entities pretty well.
Missing/incorrect elements: Relationships between entities are all 1:1, and a lot of expected relationships are missing.
Continue development?:
Yes, but together with the input list of user stories. The outputed Mendix project contains a lot of the expected entities and pages, so there is definitely a basis to continue development from. 
The downside is that there are a lot of entities/pages/relationships that will need to be removed, which can be a complex process to get right.
Needed improvements: The entity relationships would need to be much more accurate, and a lot of the entities with one or two fields should be excluded or merged with larger entities.
Additional comments: The user stories for education were well made and clear, which you could see in the Mendix output. The matching user stories had a few unclear/ambiguous points that the LLM seemed to have trouble with.

Strongest aspects: I think starting from zero is always harder than starting from something and being able to update and change that. In that sence the first steps are already present in the generated applications.
Missing/incorrect elements:
Functionality needs a lot of updates since no roles / permissions or flows have been included. In addition to this the reelationships and entities in the application are not according to the user flows and need to be revised. 
I feel like the most important things that are missing are the restrictions and logical flows for the users to follow. Since the main create and edit functionalities are there.
Continue development?: For the collaboration application I might use it to continue, although it needs a lot of updating as well. The sports application I would not since there are a lot of extra entities and additions that are not usable and might obscure the intended flows.
Needed improvements: I would focus on one concept and might keep a human in the loop for each step. First generating a correct domain model, then have that checked / altered. From there creating the user roles and permissions and then have that checked etc. For now it is building on entities that should not be there making at a lot bigger and a lot more work to delete and change stuff.

Strongest aspects: The LLM did not hallucinate when generating, meaning there was were no incorrect entities, fields or microflows and workflows. So it is very constrained, which can be nice as a developer and using this a starting point for further development.
Missing/incorrect elements:
Best practices on building Mendix application were generally ignored, such as not using enumerations for a status. Also the respective user roles were not implemented per page, so it was hard to do a thorough testing of the applications. Also, working with demo data could help.

It felt kind of random which attributes were required or not. Some important fields, like a field for a payment confirmation were either not required or missing entirely..
Continue development?: I think for small application with a small scope it would be fine, but as the context gets larger I think it become to unreliable, and you would spend a lot of time fixing existing entities, relations, and naming conventions which can be frustrating and not help with saving time.
Needed improvements: Best practices of for making a Mendix domain model, correct assignment of user roles in the navigations, and adding demo data for quick testing.
Additional comments: I think a lot of these problems could be solved by adding more context to the LLM pipeline than just a set of user stories. Adding a technical design with UML diagrams, BPMN proces models could help greatly.

Strongest aspects: The created pages are the most postive aspect of the generated applications.
Missing/incorrect elements: Correct domain model, missing or wrong associations no validations in the domain model. No description in the domain model.
Continue development?: No, because it starts with the domain model. When the domain model is not right, the mendix app is useless.
Needed improvements: Improve domain model and build from that point to a AI generated app.
Additional comments: lovely idea, maybe try AI agents.
